# Prefer Observable Systems

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)**

## The Principle

Every production engineering system MUST produce sufficient telemetry —
logs, metrics, traces, and health signals — to allow an operator to
diagnose its current behavior without modifying or redeploying the system.
This principle extends the meta-layer JOS-P06 (Observable Systems) into
concrete engineering requirements.

## Concept Foundation

- **JOS-C22 (Feedback Loop Latency)** — The value of a signal is inversely
  proportional to the delay between an event and its observation. Systems
  that lack observability extend feedback loops to infinity: the event
  occurs, but no one knows until a user reports a symptom. Engineering
  observability compresses feedback loops to seconds or minutes.
- **JOS-P06 (Observable Systems — meta layer)** — The meta-layer principle
  establishes that governed systems must be observable. This eng-domain
  principle specifies what observability means in an engineering context:
  structured logs, dimensional metrics, distributed traces, and health
  endpoints.

## Why This Matters

Unobservable systems are ungovernable systems. If you cannot see what a
service is doing, you cannot know whether it is compliant, healthy, or
drifting toward failure. Observability is not a feature added after launch;
it is a prerequisite for responsible operation. Teams that ship without
observability are shipping blind — any governance claim about that system
is an assumption, not an assertion.

## Tension and Tradeoffs

Observability has real costs: storage for logs, cardinality pressure on
metrics backends, performance overhead from tracing instrumentation. The
principle does not demand infinite telemetry; it demands enough telemetry
to diagnose behavior. Teams must balance signal density against cost and
performance. The "required" flexibility level means this is not optional —
but the depth and granularity of instrumentation are engineering judgments
that vary by system criticality.

## 1970/2070 Note

In 1970, observability meant printf to a line printer. In 2070, it may
mean continuous AI-driven anomaly detection across billions of signals.
The tools change; the requirement does not. A system that cannot be
observed cannot be understood, and a system that cannot be understood
cannot be trusted.

## Baseline+Overlay Note

This eng-domain principle extends JOS's universal governance. It operates
in addition to, not instead of, meta-layer principles. Specifically, it
provides the engineering-concrete interpretation of JOS-P06 (Observable
Systems) from the meta layer.

### Framework Connection

Framework §8 (Evidence contract as one of five boundary contracts) and §10 (slot health scoring) provide the observability mechanism — the Evidence contract defines what signals a component must emit, and health scoring aggregates those signals into system-level observability.
